meaning: vt.移置,转移,取代,置换v.转移
examples: Renewable energy will have to displace fossil fuels to a much greater extent in the future to avoid unacceptable climate risks.
Humans are the ultimate invasive species—when they move into new territory, they often displace the wildlife that was already living there.
This is because, according to Archimedes' principle of displacement, floating objects displace their own weight in water.
The problem, of course, is that tax hikes crowd out and displace spending reform.
Bombing the market is a common practice by big companies to displace smaller ones.
